subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 7
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For gridded questions, enter just the number. For fill-in-the-blank equations, enter the number that makes the equation true.

questions.questionText: What multiplication fact does this array of dots show?
questions.explanation: There are 6 rows of 5 dots: 6 × 5 = 30.

questions.questionText: Which number is shown by these base-ten blocks?
questions.explanation: 3 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones make 306.

questions.questionText: What is 84 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: 84 is closer to 80 than to 90, so it rounds to 80.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 2 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 2/6.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is almost to 5 and the minute hand points to 9, which is 45 minutes, so 4:45.

questions.questionText: What is 524 − 187? Enter just the number.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
What is 42 ÷ 6?
Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: 1/4 has fewer shaded parts than 3/4, so 1/4 < 3/4.

questions.questionText: How many sides does this shape have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has 6 sides.

questions.questionText: How many cans did Room 12 collect?
questions.explanation: The bar for Room 12 reaches 16 on the scale.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 3 equal parts and the point is at the second mark, which is 2/3.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 40 ÷ 8 = 5, so the blank is 8.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
A box holds 6 rows of crayons with 9 crayons in each row. How many crayons are in the box? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 6 × 9 = 54 crayons.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 2/4?
questions.explanation: 2/4 covers the same amount as 1/2, so they are equivalent.

questions.questionText: Art class starts at the time shown and lasts 35 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.explanation: 1:20 plus 35 minutes is 1:55.

questions.questionText: A poster is 7 feet long and 4 feet wide. What is its area, in square feet? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = 7 × 4 = 28 square feet.

questions.questionText: Each star stands for 4 votes. How many votes did Blue get?
questions.explanation: 8 stars × 4 = 32 votes.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Select all the shapes that are quadrilaterals (shapes with exactly 4 sides).
questions.explanation: A trapezoid and a rectangle each have exactly 4 sides. A hexagon has 6 and a triangle has 3.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: With the same denominator, fewer shaded parts means less: 2/8 < 5/8.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 6 equal parts and the point is at the fifth mark, which is 5/6.

questions.questionText: There are 10 marbles in a bag. 7 of them are blue. What fraction of the marbles are blue?
questions.explanation: 7 out of 10 marbles are blue, so 7/10.

questions.questionText: What is the value of the digit 5 in the number 458? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: The 5 is in the tens place, so its value is 50.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Which expression has the same value as 8 × 4?
questions.explanation: Multiplication can be done in any order, so 4 × 8 = 8 × 4 = 32.

questions.questionText: In the counting pattern 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, ..., select all the numbers that belong in the pattern.
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiples of 6. 42 and 48 are multiples of 6; 40 and 50 are not.

questions.questionText: A bag of apples has a mass of 3 kilograms. A bag of oranges has a mass of 2 kilograms. What is the total mass of both bags?
questions.explanation: 3 + 2 = 5 kilograms in all.

questions.questionText: A pitcher holds 8 liters of juice. It is poured equally into 4 jars. How many liters are in each jar?
questions.explanation: 8 ÷ 4 = 2 liters in each jar.

questions.questionText: A farmer planted 5 rows of corn with 8 plants in each row. Then 9 plants were eaten by deer. How many plants are left?
questions.explanation: 5 × 8 = 40 plants; 40 − 9 = 31 plants left.

questions.questionText: Each book costs $6. Sam buys 4 books and pays with a $40 bill. How much change does he get back?
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
What is the perimeter, in units, of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 7 + 3 + 7 + 3 = 20 units. (Area would be 21, a common trap.)

questions.questionText: A movie started at 2:45 and ended at 3:30. How long did the movie last?
questions.explanation: From 2:45 to 3:00 is 15 minutes, and 3:00 to 3:30 is 30 minutes: 15 + 30 = 45 minutes.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is the greatest?
questions.explanation: When the top number is 1, the smaller the bottom number, the larger the fraction. 1/3 is the greatest because 3 is the smallest denominator.

questions.questionText: Select all the expressions that equal 36.
Select all the fractions that are equal to a whole (1).
questions.explanation: 4/4, 3/3, and 8/8 each equal one whole because the top and bottom numbers are the same. 5/6 is less than one whole.

questions.questionText: A store had 8 packs of pencils with 9 pencils in each pack. They sold 35 pencils. How many pencils are left? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 8 × 9 = 72 pencils; 72 − 35 = 37 left.
